someone either in the house or outside you say
good morning how are you doing and the response will be I'm fine thank you how
are you okay just like that so if you meet someone in the afternoon you say
hi how are you doing and that person will say I'm fine thank you how are you
in Bemba when you meet someone in the afternoon you say
I'm someone who's fond to say simple just like that okay so don't forget to
subscribe share and like this video thank you guys see you next time

hcQSUfFHafo.title: Learn ChiBemba Beginner to fluent

segments.text: Hello, my name is Cynthia and I'm a Bemba language tutor.
Just a little bit about myself, I was born and raised in Zambia,
but I'm currently a permanent resident in the Republic of South Africa.
Among my favorite things is spending time with people.
I love talking, I love sharing ideas, thoughts and interests with others,
as well as good food with good friends.
And so, lessons with me will be centered around everyday activities,
and that is bound to make our classes very, very interesting.
Now, speaking about Bemba, Bemba is one of the most widely spoken languages in Zambia.
The Bemba tribe has its origins in the Lubalunda Empire that thrived in the 7th century in the Congo Basin.
So, at the death of the chief, one of the chief's sons, along with his followers,
migrated to Zambia and settled there.
And so, the Bemba tribe has become the most assertive culturally and influential among the people of Zambia.
And so, thank you for allowing me to guide you to the heart of this most beloved Zambian language
that you find almost in every province.
And so, I look forward to having classes with you and to guiding you as your tutor.
So, once again, as we say in Zambia, I welcome you to your class.

T5vW1zLiWeM.title: Ubuntu Talks Chibemba

segments.text: Mwishani mkwae. Nami Sengela. Ishi naliandini ne Rachel.
Hello. Welcome to Ubuntu Talks Bemba.
My name is Rachel, and I am the native Bemba-speaking instructor.
I will be taking you through the beautiful journey of learning and discovering the Bemba language.
Not only are we going to look at the language, we'll also focus on the culture of the Bemba-speaking people.
The word Bemba means a large water body, which refers to the people of the lake.
This language is spoken by over 3.8 million people in northeast Zambia, as well as the southeast of the DRC Congo.
Bemba is used in media, education, and literature.
It comprises of dialects such as Chishinga and Ichaushi.
So let's get going on this beautiful journey of learning and discovering the beautiful language Ichi Bemba.

BS55PkQW6x4.title: Zambia Language Nyanja

segments.text: The Nyanja language, also known as Chewa, is a Bantu language spoken primarily in Zambia,
Malawi, Mozambique, and Zimbabwe. It is one of the most widely spoken Bantu languages
in Central and Southern Africa. The Nyanja language belongs to the Bantu language family,
which is a subgroup of the larger Niger-Congo language family. The Bantu people are believed
to have originated in West Africa and gradually migrated east and southward over many centuries.
As part of this Bantu migration, various Bantu-speaking groups settled in different
regions, including what is now Zambia. The history of the Nyanja language is closely
tied to the history of the Chewa people, who are one of the Bantu ethnic groups that speak Nyanja.
The Chewa people are believed to have settled in the present-day Malawi-Zambia region around
the 15th century. During the colonial period in Africa, European powers, particularly the British
and Zambia, had a significant impact on the region's languages. They introduced English
as the official language and established missionary schools that taught English.
While English became the language of education and administration, Nyanja continued to be spoken
as the native language by many Zambians. Zambia is a linguistically diverse country,
with over 70 different languages spoken. While English is the official language,
Nyanja is widely spoken in the eastern and central regions of Zambia. It has also influenced
other local languages in Zambia due to its widespread use. In recent years, there has
been a growing effort to preserve and promote Nyanja as part of Zambia's cultural heritage.
Organizations, educators, and cultural advocates work to document the language,
develop educational materials, and promote Nyanja in schools and communities.
If you enjoyed this video and found it helpful,
please don't forget to subscribe to our channel, give it a thumbs up, see you in the next one.

segments.text: In Chinyanja, that means, how are you?
My name is Moses Chingala from Uwontu Talks, and I speak Bemba, Chinyanja, I speak English,
and I'm beginning to learn Tonga as well.
And you might ask me why.
Why bother learning all these languages?
Well, we live in a global village, and learning a new language exposes you to greater opportunities
to engage with different people from different cultures.
I have a master's degree, and I teach development studies at university, and I've been working
in the university environment for 12 years now.
At Uwontu Talks, I teach Chinyanja, a language that is widely spoken by urban populations
of Zambia, Malawi, Mozambique, and Zimbabwe.
Learning a new language can be very challenging, but at Uwontu, I make learning Chinyanja a
So come on, book a class with us, and start that amazing journey.

_5skwl2ubHc.title: Ubuntu Talks Chitonga

segments.text: Mwali buti nyumuse ndami tambula maningi ndili kome ni
Hello everyone, I'm very happy to receive you. My name is Mutinta, a native of Jitonga.
So Jitonga is one of the main languages that is spoken in Zambia.
We have about 73 languages in Zambia and Jitonga is one of those.
It hails from Southern Province. Southern Province is home to the town of Livingstone, which is our tourist capital.
And one of the seven natural wonders of the world, the Victoria Falls or Musi Otunya as we like to call it,
is actually the smoke that thunders in reference to the water as it hits down the falls.
So do you want to know more about Jitonga? Do you want to learn and speak like a native like me?
Well, this is your chance. I'll be your guide through learning about the Jitonga language.
By the way, did you know that Jitonga cannot be traced to any other part of Africa?
Most of the tribes in Zambia can be traced either to Central Africa, Southern Africa or even Eastern Africa.
But for the Jitonga, it's a mystery. Where did this tribe come from?
So if you want to learn more about this wonderful language, this mystery language, you can join me and become my student.
